Narrative_Meaning: Sevens signify surprises, whether in the sense of conspiracy, illnesses and light hearted fun.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Sword Card represents danger, combat and peril. The Sword is a weapon, and so it represents warfare, weaponry and attacking forces. Sometimes arguments escalate, the Sword shows us the precise moment that things escalate to High Stakes.

Significator.Plot: The Sword Plot is a War Story. It is a tale of attackers and assaults, tactics and defences.
Significator.Side: The side marked by the Sword are aggressive, angry, often dangerous (interactions with them are all one Stake Higher than normal), and when possible on the war path. They will respond to conversation with threats, and to threats with violence, physical challenges will invoke weapons and armed combat.
Significator.Character:
The Sword Significator indicates a Character who is quick to attack and deadly with it.

They Gain Yin when they are in peril.

They Gain Yang when they prepare for action.

They Gain Chi when they attack another.

They also gain +1 Success Levels to all attacks they make.
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A character defined by aggression and lethality, always poised for conflict. They thrive in moments of peril and are most comfortable when either preparing for a strike or actively engaged in battle, carrying themselves with the dangerous air of a drawn blade.
Significator.Scene: The Sword Scene is usually a fight or battle. An Opposed Ordeal at High or Extreme Stakes where lives are on the line. Although it can be used to Gain a Weapon Descendant, Military equipment of some sort, or an effective fighter Ally. Yarn-Tellers may play the Sword to inflict a military defeat, or injury on a target, or they may gain military equipment or allies.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Hitches, the bigger the better.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story hooks the character with a sudden attack. This attack should be connected (even if only peripherally) to the main Villain, so a trail of revenge would lead the PCs back. Of course, the attack on the PCs may not have been deliberate, but just be a domino chain of events.
Yarn.Gain: Lower a Hitch Boon by one or several Boons.
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: A significant recovery or healing—the lifting of a burden, the mending of a long-standing injury, or the resolution of a persistent personal struggle.
Yarn.Fray_Description: Battle is a massive, Extreme stakes Opposed Ordeal, where at least two armed forces exchange wounds. There will be deaths on both sides usually, or worse, enemies may come in waves, a steady stream, or may be separated by Stages.
Yarn.Snag_Description: A Powerful Character (often a Monster with at least TWO Monster Facets and more normally around 5) such as a Cyborg Commando, Griffin and rider, Dragon, Vampire Assassin, Chimera or Demon, who opposes the PCs. This opposition may be temporary, and the Character may turn out to be a potential Ally, but first there will be a fight. This is usually the result of a misunderstanding, that will not be resolved until after they have engaged in battle.

NPC.Description: Warriors are military men, usually heroes (although Scouts, Vandals and Gladiators are also possible). All Warriors have a "Special Move" that is drilled into them, usually a standard for that nation's military training, or their combat role.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs get to take it easy, as another side (or new Embodiment) suddenly engages the (old) Opposed Embodiment in a direct conflict (usually of raised Stakes, often of High or Extreme). The PCs old enemy may easily defeat this accidental ally, or may fall to this new threat, in which case, expect a Revelation.
Yarn.History_Description: The Sword indicates that war lies behind the current situation. Sometimes this will be the last great war, other times it will be an older conflict rearing its head again.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Sword indicates the current situation is a battle. This might be a minor skirmish, or an enormous world spanning conflict, sometimes you will be on one side, and sometimes you are trapped in between two massive armies.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Sword indicates that the current situation can be resolved with superior tactics, there may be some violence involved, and there will always be danger and threats.

Revelations.About_Description: The information revealed is about the Shadows Conflict Embodiment. All Conflicts have hidden actors and influences. This Revelation should not actually reveal the Shadows (that would make them not Shadows after all), only highlight their actions, and movements, revealing the existence of these hidden actors.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information about a Descendant, that may help or be hindering.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Maybe there's gunpowder burns on their fingers, or perhaps a smear of a specific engine grease on their glove, maybe some left over "product" has turned up on the streets. Some residue points to the information others need.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about a danger, this may be that a character is dangerous, a dangerous spell, weapon or just some kind of situational hazard that you should be aware of.
Revelations.Detail_Description: The detail is about some danger or hazard to do with the Embodiment. Perhaps the Death-Ray pistol everyone is hunting for is actually not completed and if shaken when fired can short-circuit, or perhaps the fact that the warlord just purchased a number of Kallidian Moat Vipers should make the party rethink the "We can just swim that" plan.

Obstacle.Description: A Death-Trap is a deadly obstacle that is not intended to be survived. Land-Mines, poison coated spikes, decapitating blades, quantum annihilation fields, black mist filled mirrors, and unshielded black-holes within arms length, are all example Death-Traps.

Tarot.Crossed: Target rolls a Complete Failure or Draws minimum.
Tarot.Normal: +2 Success Levels to one roll, or Ordeal Card.

Ordeal.Stage_Description: The way ahead is suspect somehow, maybe the clouds of vapour are just steam, or maybe they are poisonous. Perhaps the Stage involves tracking or finding a suspect or dealing with a suspicious package or place.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Don't stop now, their squid-riders are gaining!
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Ebbing Enemy falls-back, disengaging where possible, trying to reach a more secure position. The Flowing forces will add +1 Pips to all cards they play during Disengagements to attack.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The Fight is against a powerful enemy such as a Greater Demon or Horde of Monsters.
  Facet    Monster  
Gossamer ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Wiry — The Monster is immensely physically powerful, although often its form may not reveal it. It often demonstrates supernatural power as well as strength and toughness. A small, strong Goblin, a robot that looks like a little girl, a cybernetically enhanced rat, or even a killer doll (when not an Object Monster) are all examples of a Gossamer Monster. The Wiry Monster can add Gossamer Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever it is underestimated, cared for, or demonstrates its strength. A Wiry monster may add their current Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action with their strength or toughness. Wiries must spend cards from their Monster Pool as though it was a Prepared Defence to ignore damage.
Inertia ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Unstoppable — The Monster is inexorable, it does not stop and cannot be killed by any normal means. They start play with a Monster Pool of Inertia Score cards. Unstoppable Monsters can add Inertia Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cross a threshold, pass a barrier, or are forced to stop or turn around. Unstoppable Monsters can ignore a number of Wounds equal to the number of cards in their Monster Pool. Wounds stacked beyond the Monster Pool affect them as normal. Inexorable Monsters can also ignore all Hitches with a Boon less than their Monster Pool Size. Whenever an Unstoppable Monster is damaged beyond this limit, it must Discard the Highest Card in the Monster Pool, it may immediately also Discard any Wounds lower than discarded Monster Card, any remaining Wounds are then discarded on a one Monster Pool card per Wound basis. However, while discarding these wounds and cards the Monster must remain still (playing dead) until a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Number of Monster Pool cards Discarded (the initial card is instant, the next discard takes an action, then a second takes a few seconds, the third Wound takes an Ordeal Round, 25 Wounds would take a week). Examples of the Inertial Monster type include the Hungry Dead (Zombies) and most robotic assassins (especially ones from the future).
Key ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Hunter — The Monster is a hunter or tracker, it should favour harrying attacks and hit and run tactics. Typically such a Monster will vanish or flee if it is wounded, only to reappear when healed. The hunter may be fooled by false trails, but never for long. Hunters may add Key Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they spot a track, clue, or similar, that indicates the subject of their hunt. Hunters receive a bonus to their Score or Pips equal to the number of cards in the Monster Pool on any tracking, attacking or hunting actions if they also spend a card from the Monster Pool on the action. Hunters may convert Mortal and Carnage Wounds that they take into Crippling Wounds instead, at a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. In T13, the archetypal version of this Monster is a Bulmäs Pack member from the Wild Hunt, but any Werewolf or Animal Shifter would probably qualify, as would robot cowboys in theme parks that have gone wrong, and any fantasy world’s Elven ranger.
Miasma ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Mutant — The monster is a mutant or chimerical creature made from parts of others. This monster type is the oldest perhaps, you’ll find examples throughout the ancient world, particularly in Greek myth. The Mutant will often be larger, and may be malformed or unsymmetrical, with odd numbers of limbs, eyes, horns, or even heads. The Chimera will blend multiple animals together to create a new creature that combines features of all the “parent” creatures, preserving the best of all species. Mutant Monsters start with Miasma Score Monster Pool. They may add Miasma Draw cards to the Pool every time they accomplish a specified goal, such as eating a human, consuming a soul, bathing in radiation, or just growing a year older, etc. The Monster Pool can be used as a “Mutation” Annex, which can be used to mimic the abilities of the Mutants “parent” creatures, such as a spider’s ability to climb, or an eagle’s ability to fly. The Mutation Annex has a Boon equal to the Value of the Number of Cards in the Monster Pool, and so draws cards equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced and plays a number of cards equal to the Miasma Draw. The Mutation Annex is often used as a Size Annex for the Mutant. Where a 5 card Monster Pool would create a Boon 16 Annex, and could grant a Kaiju Mutant the size of a Skyscraper. 1950s B-movie monsters are often of this type, giant ants, lizards, spiders, wasps, and even rabbits have been used. Classically of course, Cyclopses, Centaurs, Minotaurs, Satyrs, Gryphons (or Griffins), Hippogriffs, Dragons, Sphinxes, Manticores and of course the Chimera itself are all examples. You can also find examples in superheroes, especially those created by radioactive arachnids, or those with an X-gene.
Trial ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Killer — The Killer Monster is a deadly Combat specialist of some sort. Killers add Trial Draw cards to their Monster Pool each time they kill. Killers automatically add +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to all attacks, and Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) is automatically added to the Pips of any attack as Penetration. Killers must spend Monster Pool cards whenever they will make a Trick either with other cards being played by the Ordeal Pool, or other cards in the Round Pool or a Style Reserve. Killers can be drug-addled assassins, crazed shooters, or just steel-souled soldiers.
Yonder ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Alien — The Monster is an alien, an outsider. It may just be a metaphor for alienation, or literally be an extra-(or ultra)-terrestrial. Such monsters are usually obvious and inhuman, not just to look at, but in the way they think and behave. They may have apparently supernatural powers and abilities, but often make do with advanced technologies (in the form of additional Descendants). They may add Yonder Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they are observed doing their alien / inhuman / weird actions. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any action to do with their alien abilities, technologies, motion or survival (including Dodge, Soak, etc). Alien Monsters must play cards from their Monster Pool to socialise with “non-aliens”. From little grey Eusaiveans, through pod people and pepper-pot shaped engines of hate, all the way to face-hugging space-wasps, there are plenty of aliens.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Bodypart: Hunting Senses (Binocular Vision Eyes / Scent Tracking nose)
Lea.Notes: Traditionally the most common animal path in human occupied territory.

Stress.Description: Stress makes things harder. Things that we could previously bear with grace become impossible to cope with, and all too often the Stress interferes with our skills and talents. Stress can even make us revel in these hardships, justifying and internalising the failures.
Stress.Rules: All Spades suited Actions (Gossamer, Inertia, Key, Miasma, Trial or Yonder) add Difficulty equal to the Character’s current Stress in Intrepid Facet Sway. If a penalised Action fails the Character may reduce Stress by 1.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can make a Character more dangerous, quicker to anger, and more likely to be carrying a deadly weapon.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma can Relieve all current Stress, but creates a "Sword" Pool from the Stress. The Character must keep a weapon Descendant with a cost at least equal to the Sword Pool near them at all times, and can Relieve 1 Stress by using this weapon at High Stakes.

Age.Description: An Age of War is occurring. During this age a society engages in warfare with another society. Wars come in various types, from the chaotic and bloody, firey mess that is total war, to the relative paranoid quiet of a cold war, or anything in between. All wars contain battles, which are always bloody wastes of lives for both societies, although during cold wars often proxy societies will sacrifice the most, fighting direct, but proxy battles for their cold war allies.
Age.Diegetic_Description: A violent and transformative era where the drumbeats of conflict drown out all other concerns. Whether it is a slow, paranoid struggle or a swift, fiery conflagration, society is entirely reoriented toward the machinery of warfare and the harsh reality of strategic survival.
Age.Rules: During an Age of War a society is engaged in armed (and often armoured) comabt against at least one other society. Wars are complex strategic Ordeals that rely on logistics, intelligence, equipment ease of maintenance, long-term morale and discipline more than actual battlefield prowess.
